translated unsupported instruction reaches an installed handler while default SIGILL still terminates
synchronous translated load faults expose the exact guest instruction rather than the block start
SA_RESTART timer handler must run before an interrupted wait() is restarted (timeout-style preemption)
an uncaught fatal-default signal must not leak an engine diagnostic to the guest stderr fd
SIGURG async-preempt is dropped for EVERY aarch64 Go image (build-info magic), not only the cgo class -- covers the internal-linked go build toolchain children
SA_NODEFER lets a signal re-enter its own handler while the default disposition auto-masks it
SA_RESETHAND reverts to SIG_DFL after one delivery so a second raise takes the default action
sigtimedwait returns EAGAIN on timeout and immediately for an already-pending signal
a non-SA_RESTART handler leaves an interrupted read returning EINTR
nanosleep is never restarted by SA_RESTART; it returns EINTR with the unslept remainder
poll is never restarted by SA_RESTART; an interrupting signal returns EINTR
sigsuspend returns EINTR and atomically installs then restores the prior signal mask
a SIGCHLD handler draining waitpid(WNOHANG) reaps every child despite standard-signal coalescing
SA_NOCLDSTOP suppresses SIGCHLD on child stop/continue but still delivers it on exit
synchronous SIGSEGV from a NULL store exposes SEGV_MAPERR and si_addr==NULL, recovered via siglongjmp
writing to a pipe with a closed read end raises SIGPIPE and returns EPIPE; SIG_IGN suppresses the signal
send(MSG_NOSIGNAL) to a closed-peer socket returns EPIPE without raising SIGPIPE
a signal raised inside a handler nests strictly, running to completion before the outer handler resumes
uncaught fatal signals terminate the child with WTERMSIG matching the delivered signal
abort raises SIGABRT; a returning handler still dies by default, a handler that exits proves it ran
pidfd_send_signal delivers a signal via a pidfd; signal 0 probes liveness
pthread_sigmask makes a process-directed signal route to the one thread that leaves it unblocked
kill signal 0 probes liveness, an unused pid gives ESRCH, an out-of-range signo gives EINVAL
sigaction reports current disposition via oldact and rejects catching SIGKILL/SIGSTOP with EINVAL
sigprocmask returns the prior mask in oldset, queries with NULL, and SIG_SETMASK replaces wholesale
pause returns EINTR after a caught signal's handler runs
signal() returns the previous disposition and rejects SIGKILL with SIG_ERR/EINVAL
pthread_kill to self runs the handler on this thread; signal 0 is a validity probe
sigsetjmp saves the mask so siglongjmp out of a handler restores the pre-handler signal mask
distinct per-signal handlers each fire once per raise and route correctly
a child inherits the blocked signal mask across fork so a raised signal stays pending
waitpid(WNOHANG) returns 0 for a live child, reaps it after exit, and ECHILD with no children
a blocked standard signal is delivered exactly once at the moment it is unblocked
